reasoning: John Morgan has been very publicly 'flirting' with a 2026 gubernatorial run, going as far as laying out a blueprint and announcing the creation of a new political party (possibly the 'Capitalist Party') in February 2025. A critical factor is Florida's 365-day party affiliation rule, which requires a candidate to be a member of their party exactly one year before the qualifying deadline (June 12, 2026). This sets an effective deadline of June 12, 2025, for Morgan to establish his party and register as its member if he wishes to run under its banner. As of the knowledge cutoff of June 6, 2025, no formal announcement has been made, but the impending legal deadline provides a strong incentive for an announcement in the subsequent 6 days. However, Morgan's history of teasing runs without following through (such as in 2018) and his public statements about being 'uncertain' and believing his brand allows him to wait longer than other candidates suggest a high risk that he may again decide against a run or simply miss the third-party deadline, opting for a later No Party Affiliation (NPA) bid or no bid at all. Given the proximity of the June 12 deadline to the July 1 resolution date and the significant media attention on his 'blueprint,' the probability is slightly higher than a coin flip, but tempered by his past behavior.
